author: @AC23503
text:
The show, and then by extension the character, Daredevil, saved my life. I was first introduced to the character through the show and became so enamored with it, I went and read through his entire comic roster from 1964 to now.

I have been an addict and was suicidal for the longest time and what I loved so much about daredevil was how much I related to him. That even though he had some tough things happen to him (Born Again as a prime example) he still persevered and never gave up and continued to believe in justice.

This character means so much to me and to so many people because he provides me with so much hope. I’m happy to say I’ve turned my life around and I’m not in such a low state anymore. But I’m looking forward to the show BECAUSE of how much Daredevil meant to me and helped inspire me to overcome my afflictions and be better. I know I’m rooting for the show to succeed because it is always a great reminder to me to never give up.

replies.author: @RabbiSteve1
replies.text: That is a great story and reason to love this character and to look forward to the show. I hope it continues to be what you need in recovery

replies.text: Hell yeah! I'm glad you found something that was a beacon to a better life. Growing up Catholic and an Altar Boy (No, no priest ever touched me. In fact, one saved my life when I was suicidal.) Matt Murdoch was, is, and always will be my favorite superhero. I hope your success and happiness continues to grow with each day.

replies.author: @AC23503
@davidfinch7407
replies.text: Tipping my hat to you, sir. This is why cultural vandalism to our fictional heroes matters. Superman, Batman, Thor, Luke Skywalker, Captain Kirk- maybe they are silly on some level, but they also inspire us. We like them, admire them, and to some extent, we want to be like them. When Thor has even his very name stolen from him by his girlfriend- what chance do us mere mortals have? When Hulk is too scared to come out and fight- that discourages us from fighting, too. When someone like Rey Palpatine becomes the bestest Jedi ever, better than Luke, without his training and practice, with no heroes journey- well, then, why should we practice hard to succeed? But when we see our heroes try, fail, and try again- we can have the strength to try again, too. Well done, AC, well done.
